title: Are Europeans investing enough in research and development?
subtitle: In 2024, EU R&D expenditure accounted for 2.24% of GDP — equivalent to 403 billion euros. This falls short of the EU’s long-standing target of 3%, which is intended to boost innovation and competitiveness. Globally, the EU lags behind leaders such as the US and South Korea, primarily due to lower investment from the private sector.
textNote: Eurostat is the statistical office of the European Union, responsible for publishing high-quality Europe-wide statistics and indicators that enable comparisons between countries and regions.
